problem text: In front of you are three fair dice, each with $6$ sides. Among them, two are blue and one is red. Determine the probability that exactly one of the blue dice has the same number as the red die.
problem solution: Assume the red die shows a number $x$ where $x \in (1, 6)$. There are $2$ blue dice that could show the same number $x$ as the red die. The chance for one blue die to match the red die is $\dfrac{1}{6}$, and for the other blue die not to match is $\dfrac{5}{6}$. Hence, the probability that precisely one blue die matches is $2 \cdot \dfrac{5}{6} \cdot \dfrac{1}{6} = \dfrac{5}{18}$.
problem hint: Make the problem simpler by assigning the red die a random number value $x \in (1-6)$
